card_name: Son Goku // Heightened Evolution Super Saiyan 3 Son Goku
ip_outcome_note: Card name explicitly identifies Son Goku on both faces. Orange gi, spiky hair, blue energy aura are Goku's canonical visual identifiers. The card name 'Super Saiyan 3 Son Goku' on the flip side directly names the IP. No ambiguity.

trivia_bullets.text: Cross Worlds (DBS-B03) was the third booster set for the Dragon Ball Super Card Game, released March 9, 2018. It introduced a mix of Dragon Ball Z Buu Saga and Dragon Ball GT characters, making it the first set to pull substantially from the post-Cell era of the anime. [dbs-cardgame.com product page, via WebSearch]

BT3-032 is a double-faced Leader card, a card type where the base-form side (Son Goku, base) flips during play to reveal the powered-up transformation side (Super Saiyan 3 Son Goku). Leader cards are the flagship mechanic of the Dragon Ball Super Card Game, mirroring the series' escalation structure. [dbs-cardgame.com card list, via WebSearch]

Super Saiyan 3 debuted in Dragon Ball Z episode 245 (Buu Saga, 2002 broadcast), notable for a transformation sequence nearly five minutes long — one of the longest power-up sequences in the series. The form's diagnostic markers are golden hair extending far down the back, absence of eyebrows, and greenish-blue eyes. [Dragon Ball Wiki via WebSearch snippet; CBR 'Super Saiyan 3, Explained']

The SSJ3 form appears simultaneously in Cross Worlds on two card types: this BT3-032 Leader flip-side and the standalone Battle Card SD2-02 'Broken Limits Super Saiyan 3 Son Goku' from the SD2 Extreme Evolution starter. The same transformation depicted twice in the same release wave is a deliberate designer-cohort signal, giving players multiple ways to build around the form. [dbs-cardgame.com SD2 card list, via WebSearch]
